from target import parse_ipv4

def test_parse_ipv4():
    # normal case
    assert parse_ipv4("192.168.0.1") == (192, 168, 0, 1)
    # edge case: maximum values
    assert parse_ipv4("255.255.255.255") == (255, 255, 255, 255)
    # error cases
    import pytest
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        parse_ipv4("10.0.0")  # not enough octets
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        parse_ipv4("10.0.0.256")  # octet out of range
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        parse_ipv4("01.2.3.4")  # leading zero not allowed
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        parse_ipv4("a.b.c.d")  # non‑digit octet
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        parse_ipv4("")  # empty string, not 4 octets

from target import parse_ipv4
import pytest

def test_parse_ipv4_various_cases():
    # valid addresses
    assert parse_ipv4("0.0.0.0") == (0, 0, 0, 0)
    assert parse_ipv4("127.0.0.1") == (127, 0, 0, 1)
    assert parse_ipv4("255.255.255.255") == (255, 255, 255, 255)

    # edge cases and errors
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        parse_ipv4("")                     # empty string
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        parse_ipv4("1.2.3")                # too few octets
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        parse_ipv4("1.2.3.4.5")            # too many octets
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        parse_ipv4("01.2.3.4")             # leading zero
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        parse_ipv4("256.0.0.1")            # out of range
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        parse_ipv4("a.b.c.d")              # non‑digit characters

    # ensure no hidden mutable cache influences subsequent calls
    first = parse_ipv4("10.20.30.40")
    second = parse_ipv4("50.60.70.80")
    assert first == (10, 20, 30, 40)
    assert second == (50, 60, 70, 80)
    assert id(first) != id(second)
    again = parse_ipv4("10.20.30.40")
    assert again == (10, 20, 30, 40)
    assert id(again) != id(first)

from target import parse_ipv4

def test_parse_ipv4():
    # Normal case
    assert parse_ipv4("192.168.0.1") == (192, 168, 0, 1)
    # Edge case: maximum valid octet values
    assert parse_ipv4("255.255.255.255") == (255, 255, 255, 255)
    # Invalid inputs raise ValueError
    import pytest
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        parse_ipv4("10.0.0")          # not enough octets
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        parse_ipv4("10.0.0.0.1")      # too many octets
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        parse_ipv4("01.2.3.4")        # leading zero
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        parse_ipv4("256.0.0.0")       # octet out of range
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        parse_ipv4("a.b.c.d")         # non‑digit characters
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        parse_ipv4("...")             # empty octets
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        parse_ipv4("")                # empty string
    # Zero octet is allowed
    assert parse_ipv4("0.0.0.0") == (0, 0, 0, 0)
